90.reason: [Condition should be delV >= 0 to handle deletion of vertex 0, but problem states vertex 0 is never deleted. However, more critically, in first call delV=0, so this skips marking Visited[0]=1, but the real issue is that first call should use delV=-1. This condition also fails to mark deletion when delV==0, but since delV=0 is incorrectly passed, it leads to wrong behavior. The core error is in main(), but this condition is inconsistent with BFS which uses delV>=0.]

This condition also fails to mark deletion when delV==0, but since delV=0 is incorrectly passed, it leads to wrong behavior. reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition should be delV >= 0 to handle deletion of any non-negative vertex, but excludes delV == 0. However, problem states deleted vertex is not 0, so this may be acceptable, but inconsistent with BFS which uses delV >= 0]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [condition should be delV >= 0 to handle deletion of any non-negative vertex including 0, but problem states deleted vertex is not 0; however, logic inconsistency remains because main passes 0 for "no deletion", so this condition fails to mark deletion when delV==0. But since problem says deleted vertex 鈮 0, the real bug is in main passing 0 instead of -1. This line's condition is also flawed because it skips deletion when delV==0, but main incorrectly uses 0 to mean "no delete". The correct fix is both: main passes -1, and this uses delV >= 0.]
